key_insight: Detonation-sprayed AlCuFe quasicrystalline coatings form a thermally hardened, oxidation-resistant barrier at 450°C, even as the original icosahedral phase gradually transforms into intermetallic approximants.
engineering_relevance: The coating offers a promising protective layer for TC4 titanium alloys in long-term high-temperature service where oxidation-driven degradation is a concern.
method: Detonation-sprayed AlCuFe QC coating fabricated on TC4 substrate
Aged in air at 450°C for up to 250 h
Tracked oxidation kinetics, phase evolution, and microhardness
evidence_signal: Oxidation followed a parabolic law with kp = 1.83 × 10^-2 mg^2·cm^-4·h^-1, while surface hardness increased from 491 HV0.2 to 665.2 HV0.2 after 250 h.
watch_item: Performance depends on phase evolution and Al depletion during service, so long-term stability beyond 250 h and in more severe environments still needs verification.
paper_title: High-temperature oxidation kinetics and microstructural evolution of detonation-sprayed AlCuFe quasicrystalline coatings
